data.title: Ahead of Marvell's (MRVL) Q4 Results, Options Traders Brace for 11% Price Swing
data.description:
Marvell Technology (MRVL) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results after market close today. Analysts expect earnings and sales to rise in the January quarter, driven by custom AI silicon programs, key partnerships with major cloud providers like Amazon Web Services (AMZN), Google (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT), and robust demand for AI and data center chips. Investors will also watch for updates on Marvell's planned $3.25 billion acquisition of AI startup Celestial AI.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Marvell designs semiconductors for data infrastructure solutions. MRVL stock has fallen 19.3% over the past three months amid a broader tech sell-off tied to AI disruption fears. Notably, MRVL stock also pays a quarterly dividend of $0.06 per share.

Expectations for Marvell

The Street forecasts adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.79, higher than the prior year's $0.60 per share. Sales are forecast to jump 20.8% year-over-year to $2.20 billion. It is worth noting that the company has beaten earnings estimates in six of the past eight quarters.

Moreover, according to TipRanks' Options Traders Tool, options traders expect a 11.14% move in either direction for MRVL stock in reaction to Q4 results. This implied move is higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 7.10% over the past four quarters.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: "It May Not Have Been My Wisest Tweet": Elon Musk Defends Post at Center of Twitter Deal Lawsuit
data.description:
Elon Musk told a San Francisco jury this week that a key tweet during his 2022 bid to buy Twitter was not meant to move the stock price and was probably not his wisest tweet. The post is now at the center of a lawsuit from investors who claim the message caused them losses.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The dispute dates back to Musk's $44 billion deal to buy Twitter in April 2022, before the company changed its name to X. At the time, Musk agreed to pay $54.20 per share and signed a binding deal with the company's board.

Soon after that deal was signed, Musk began to question the number of spam and bot accounts on the site. Then, on May 13, he posted on Twitter that the deal was "temporarily on hold" until the firm could prove that fake accounts made up less than 5% of users.

The message quickly moved the market.

When trading opened that day, Twitter shares fell about 9%. Over the next few weeks, the stock continued to slide and later traded just above $30. Some investors sold their shares at those lower prices. However, the deal later closed at the full price of $54.20 per share. The investors who sold early say Musk's posts misled the market and caused them to miss out on the higher payout.

Musk Says Tweet Was Not Meant to Move Markets

In court, Musk said the tweet was not meant as a sign that the deal might fail. Instead, he told the jury he was simply raising concerns about bots on the platform.

"It may not have been my wisest tweet," Musk said during testimony. "I am not sure I would call it incredibly stupid… but if it led to this trial, it probably qualifies as such."

Musk also explained that the phrase "on hold" was meant in a basic way. He compared it to saying he might be late to a meeting rather than canceling it.

He also said he did not think about how traders would read the post before sending it. The tweet was written early in the morning, and he did not review it with advisers first.

Musk added that stock markets often react in ways that are hard to predict. He told the court that markets can behave like a "manic depressive," meaning sharp swings can occur even without clear reasons.

Investors Point to Banker Emails

Meanwhile, lawyers representing the investors say the tweet was part of a plan to gain leverage in the deal talks. They showed the jury emails from Barclays bankers sent to Musk before the tweet was posted.

One message suggested that raising questions about bots could create "an opportunity to revisit price." The banker added that the threat of walking away from the deal might push Twitter to accept a lower offer.

After Musk's tweet, another message from Barclays said investors now gave the deal "50/50 odds." The email also said the stock might stay low because traders feared more tweets that could affect the deal. Musk replied to that message with a short note that said, "Matches my understanding of things."

Still, Musk denied the claim that he tried to move the stock price. He said he was simply speaking his mind about issues with the platform. The jury will now decide whether the tweets crossed a legal line. If the investors win the case, damages could reach billions of dollars.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Trade Desk Stock (TTD) Pops on Potential OpenAI Deal
data.description:
Trade Desk (TTD) stock surged about 15% in Thursday's extended trading session after The Information, citing sources, reported that OpenAI held early talks with the company for a partnership to grow the AI startup's ad business. Trade Desk offers ad buyers a cloud-based platform to create and optimize their digital advertising campaigns across ad formats and devices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Potential Partnership Between Trade Desk and OpenAI

The Information noted that while OpenAI plans to develop in-house ad-tech functions eventually, the potential collaboration with Trade Desk suggests the AI firm might rely on external partners in the meantime to boost its ad business. OpenAI launched ads in ChatGPT in February 2026.

Advertising could prove vital for OpenAI, as the company aims to monetize its user base of 920 million. The AI startup expects advertising to help double its revenue from its consumer ChatGPT business to $17 billion in 2026. Also, ads could become more crucial for OpenAI as it has reportedly dropped its plans to roll out e-commerce directly within ChatGPT.

Meanwhile, a deal with OpenAI could help revive Trade Desk's business. TTD stock has plunged 34% so far in 2026 as investors are concerned about the company's uneven performance. Moreover, fears that new tools from Anthropic and OpenAI will impact traditional software companies have also weighed on investor sentiment.

TTD stock took a major hit in late February when the ad-tech platform announced a disappointing first-quarter outlook. Notably, the company's Q1 adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) guidance of about $195 million significantly lagged the Street's $223 million estimate.

Is TTD a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Trade Desk stock based on 16 Buys, 14 Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average TTD stock price target of $35.46 indicates 41% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Down Today, 3/5/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down ahead of Thursday's session as investors watched new military developments in the Middle East amid the expanding conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.33%, 0.40%, and 0.30%, respectively, at 2:47 a.m. EST on March 5.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the day, Wall Street had already posted solid gains. The rebound was driven by strength in big tech and semiconductor stocks. The Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq rose 0.5%, 0.8%, and 1.3%, respectively.

Leading the gains were semiconductor stocks. Nvidia (NVDA) shares rose more than 1%. Other chipmakers, including Broadcom (AVGO), Micron Technology (MU), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC), also posted gains. Meanwhile, consumer staples, energy, and materials were the only S&P 500 sectors that ended the day in negative territory.

Investors now turn their attention to several major earnings reports due tomorrow. Kroger (KR), Burlington (BURL), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ) report Thursday morning, while Costco (COST) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) release results after the close. Weekly jobless claims are also due Thursday, adding another data point to a busy market week.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Big Tech Signs Landmark AI Energy Pledge – Why This Matters for Investors
data.description:
Major U.S. tech companies, including Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), Oracle (ORCL), OpenAI, and xAI, have signed a landmark AI-driven energy pledge at the White House. The initiative aims to accelerate the use of AI in energy efficiency and clean power at a time when the Trump administration is focused on curbing inflation.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last month, President Donald Trump called on Big Tech to sign the "Ratepayer Protection Pledge." The pledge is not legally binding, but administration officials say it could still encourage companies to manage their growing energy needs and ease pressure on the power grid.

What the Pledge Actually Requires

Under the terms confirmed by White House officials, participating companies must secure or directly develop the electricity for their data centers, rather than relying on the public utility grid used by households and small businesses. In addition, they must fund upgrades to local power transmission systems so that connecting new data centers does not burden the broader grid. Lastly, they must negotiate special electricity rate agreements with local utilities and states — and critically, they commit to paying those rates whether or not they actually use the power.

Companies also pledged to hire and train workers from within the communities where data centers are built, addressing a common grievance that AI facilities create relatively few permanent local jobs once construction is complete. Energy Secretary Chris Wright called the effort a commitment to "more affordable, reliable, and secure energy for the American people."

Why This Matters for Investors

For investors, the stakes go beyond policy pledges. The single largest risk for tech giants aiming to transform global computing infrastructure has always been energy. By agreeing to source their own power, these companies are committing to spending more to eliminate the energy shortage themselves.

The move paves a clearer path for data center expansion—and for the AI revenue investors are betting on. Additionally, the pledge could reduce operational uncertainty for hyperscalers whose growth depends on bringing power-hungry facilities online quickly.

For holders of META, GOOGL, MSFT, and AMZN, the pledge removes a significant regulatory and reputational overhang. It signals that Washington is willing to let the AI buildout proceed at full speed—as long as Big Tech covers its own energy costs. This clears political runway for the huge capital spending cycle that underpins analyst bull cases for these stocks.

Which Is the Best Tech Stock, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' stock comparison tool, investors can compare the above-mentioned and conduct further research to find the most promising options based on analyst ratings and insights. Among them, ORCL stock stands out with the highest upside potential of 82% and a Strong Buy rating from analysts. By comparison, GOOGL stock carries the lowest upside of 25.4% among these stocks. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Nvidia Pauses H200 Chip Production for China, Redirects TSMC to Vera Rubin
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has halted production of its China-bound H200 AI chips amid uncertainty over U.S. export approvals and rising geopolitical tensions, according to The Financial Times. The American chip giant has redirected contract manufacturer Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) to prioritize its next-gen Vera Rubin hardware instead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This move signals Nvidia's low expectations for near-term China revenue from its second-most advanced China-compliant H200 chips, prompting a reallocation to more advanced AI systems. Yesterday, Bloomberg reported that U.S. officials were considering caps on H200 chip exports to any single Chinese company.

Nvidia's Shifting AI Priorities

The news comes as a surprise since Nvidia recently got U.S. government permission to send "small batches" of its H200 AI chips to China. A U.S. Commerce Department official has also stated that no H200 chips have been sold to Chinese buyers yet, showing ongoing roadblocks for exporting advanced chips.

The H200 chips are designed to power large-scale AI tasks like training and running advanced generative AI models. Their role in high-performance computing (HPC) and national security has put them under strict U.S. export rules. Vera Rubin is Nvidia's upcoming post-Blackwell AI platform slated for 2026 production, promising massive leaps in inference performance for next-gen data centers.

Nvidia is pivoting toward future AI technologies amid stringent global semiconductor export rules. This decision highlights how chip supply chains drive the worldwide AI race, intertwining technological advances, government policies, and trade regulations.

What Is Nvidia's Price Target?

Despite the China hiccups, analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $271.89 implies 48.5% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged 56%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: BitMine Stock Soars 8% as Ethereum Price Defies the War Fog & Tom Lee Aggressively Piles into the $9.1 Billion Vault
data.description:
The crypto world is moving fast as big investors turn the current war fog into a chance to buy more assets. BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) saw its stock price climb almost 8% after sharing a big update on its digital vault. This jump happened because the company showed it is aggressively buying Ethereum (ETH-USD) even while the conflict in the Middle East makes others nervous.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tom Lee, the Chairman of BitMine, has led this push, viewing the market dip as a perfect time to build the company's reserves. At the same time, the price of Ethereum has stayed strong, rising 3.81% over the last five days to reach $2,122. Expanding its $9.1 billion treasure chest during a crisis proves that BitMine can succeed no matter what the headlines say.

Tom Lee Buys Big Even in a Scary World

While many people were worried about the war, BitMine was busy shopping for more digital money. The company's latest report shows that it now holds 4,473,587 Ethereum tokens, which are worth about $9.1 billion today.

Just one week ago, that pile was smaller, which means they bought over 50,000 new tokens in a very short time. Chairman Tom Lee is overseeing this strategy, as BitMine now owns about 3.71% of all the Ethereum in the world. This plan makes BitMine work like a giant money box that grows in value every time Ethereum's price rises.

Why Investors Are Ignoring the Warning Signs

Even though the business looks strong, some technical models are waving a red flag. If you look at the Technical Analysis tool for BitMine stock on TipRanks, the math actually shows a Strong Sell signal. There are 15 Bearish signals suggesting the price might fall, compared to only one Bullish signal. Looking closer at the breakdown:

  • Oscillators are flashing a Sell signal, with 3 Bearish, 6 Neutral, and 1 Bullish indicator.
  • Moving Averages show an even grimmer Strong Sell consensus, with 12 Bearish indicators and zero Neutral or Bullish ones.

Despite these technical warnings, investors seem to be looking at the actual price charts instead. As long as people hope for peace and Ethereum keeps its 5-day winning streak, these Strong Sell warnings are being drowned out by BitMine's aggressive buying under Chairman Tom Lee's leadership.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: The Structural Margin Bear Case for Dell Is Beginning to Crack
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) reported results for its fiscal fourth quarter that left markets clearly pleased, particularly in terms of margins and guidance for the coming fiscal year. As a result, shares rallied by double digits following the Q4 release.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition to a strong beat across the board, Dell managed to ease concerns about structural margin compression stemming from its AI server business model — a segment often perceived as having limited pricing power. Signs of a re-acceleration in operating margins within its Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), combined with a constructive outlook for this dynamic to continue in the coming quarters, place prior concerns firmly in the realm of mix rather than structural impairment to the model itself.

In other words, what initially looked like permanent dilution now appears more like transitional friction during an aggressive AI ramp. Acknowledging that this represents a meaningful reduction in risk — and that it potentially opens the door for Dell to trade at a structurally higher multiple, perhaps without a persistent discount — the thesis remains constructive. Considering bottom-line growth expectations and the ongoing tailwinds from operating leverage, I continue to view the stock with a bullish stance.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Rallies as U.S. Demand for Military-Grade AI Jumps amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
Palantir (PLTR) is seeing a lot of interest as the world becomes more unstable. On March 5, the company's stock price jumped by about 4%, continuing a strong winning streak. This latest rise comes as investors realize that Palantir is becoming the go-to partner for the U.S. government, especially as other tech companies struggle with military rules. While the broader market is rebounding, Palantir is standing out because it is winning the battle for government trust.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Government Ban on Rivals Clears the Path for Palantir

A major reason for the stock's rise is a shift in who the government chooses to work with. Recently, the Trump administration ordered agencies to stop using tools from Anthropic. This happened after Anthropic refused to remove safety rules that blocked its AI from being used for certain military tasks.

The data shows that Palantir is perfectly positioned to pick up the pieces. Palantir already runs Maven Smart Systems, a platform the military uses to analyze data and pick targets. Because Palantir is willing to work closely with the Pentagon without the same restrictions as its rivals, experts believe the company will win even more billion-dollar contracts this year.

Strong Earnings and Massive New Deals Support PLTR's Surge

It isn't just news about war driving the stock; the company's bank account is growing fast too. Palantir recently reported that its revenue from the U.S. government grew by 66% in the last quarter.

New reports indicate that the Department of Homeland Security just awarded Palantir a deal worth up to $1 billion. This five-year agreement allows multiple government agencies, like the Secret Service and FEMA, to buy Palantir's software much faster. This pre-approved status means Palantir can grow its business without having to fight for every single new project, making its future income much more predictable.

The key takeaway is that Palantir has chosen a side. While other Silicon Valley companies are worried about how their AI might be used in war, Palantir has made it clear that it is here to help the U.S. military.

Is Palantir a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Palantir Technologies stock based on 14 Buy, four Hold, and two Sell recommendations. The average 12-month PLTR stock price target of $191.76 indicates an upside potential of 25.2% from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani
